# Webster 1828 references in our studies

**Source:** every `study/**/*.md` file (excluding `.scratch/`)
**Method:** Python tokenization of citation windows around "Webster 1828" mentions; words extracted only from explicit markup (bold/italic/quotes/headings)
**Date:** 2026-05-20
**Provenance:** generated by `research/gospel/1828/.work/extract_webster_citations.py` and `compose_p1_table.py`

**Counts:** 373 raw rows · 311 attributed · 202 unique words · 311 confirmed citations across 56 distinct study files

**Tag legend:**
- **differ** — the citation context names a difference between 1828 and modern usage (or recovers a sense modern English has lost). These are the highest-value highlight candidates.
- **reinforce** — the citation context says 1828 matches or sharpens the Restoration sense. Confirms an existing reading rather than overturning one.
- **neutral** — the citation defines a word but the context doesn't explicitly frame it as differ/reinforce.

**Heuristic limitations to acknowledge:**
- The differ/reinforce tag is keyword-based; final tier curation in `00-FINAL-highlight-candidates.md` revisits each manually.
- Generic words (`why`, `from`, `both`, `through`, etc.) leak through when they appear in markup near a Webster citation. P5 synthesis filters them.
- 62 of 373 citation rows had no extractable word; usually quoted-scripture-only blocks adjacent to a real citation. Counted but not tabulated below.

## Intelligence study — the case Michael flagged

`intelligence` lensed in 3 studies (4 citations), tag: **differ**

Studies:
- [`study/morgan-philpot-revised/00_plan.md`](../../../study/morgan-philpot-revised/00_plan.md)
- [`study/plan-of-salvation/notes-01-premortal.md`](../../../study/plan-of-salvation/notes-01-premortal.md)
- [`study/truth.md`](../../../study/truth.md)

Sample citation excerpt: ***Key words to study (Webster 1828 + modern comparison):** - **Intelligence** — D&C 93:36 "the glory of God is intelligence" (already studied in [intelligence.md](../intelligence.md)) - **Receive** — what does it mean to "receive" Christ? (already studied in [receive.md](../receive.md))*

This is the case where 1828's *"the intelligent, immaterial and immortal part of man"* recovers a sense modern English loses — modern "intelligence" is cognitive ability; 1828 (and D&C 93) treat it as the substance of being itself. The matter-spectrum study (`truth.md`) makes this explicit.

---
